lock.setup.hint: Hello! Create a PIN to keep your accounts secure — you'll use it each time to unlock the app.
lock.setup.keystoreWarning: The OS keychain isn't available here — your PIN is the only thing protecting the vault.
lock.unlock.hint: Enter your PIN to pick up where you left off.
lock.error.tooShort: Your PIN needs to be at least 4 characters.
lock.error.mismatch: Those PINs don't match — try again.
lock.error.tooMany: Too many wrong attempts — please wait {secs}s.
autofill.title: Sign in to {nickname}
autofill.hint: Enter your PIN to fill in the login details. You'll still need to handle any captcha or 2FA, and click Log In yourself.
autofill.error.noPassword: No saved password found for this account.
share.title: Share your screen
share.empty: No screens or windows are available to share.
empty.noAccount: No account selected — pick one from the sidebar, or wake a hibernated account.
empty.intro: Keep all your Discord accounts running at once, each connected and notifying in the background.
settings.title: Settings
settings.general.ptt.hint: holds the mic closed while Discord is in voice-activity mode
settings.general.activateSound.hint: file path — leave blank to use the built-in sound
settings.general.deactivateSound.hint: file path — leave blank to use the built-in sound
settings.general.pttStatus.fallback: Falling back to focused-window key capture until global capture is available.
settings.general.autoHibernate.hint: frees RAM; hibernated accounts won't notify
settings.general.resourcesNote: Active accounts stay connected in the background at all times; only the one you're looking at is rendered. Hibernating an account is the only way to reclaim its memory — but you'll stop receiving its notifications.
settings.account.noAccounts: You don't have any accounts yet. Add one from the sidebar.
settings.account.chime.hint: file path — leave blank for the default blip
settings.account.proxy.hint: e.g. socks5://host:port — for privacy, not ban evasion
settings.account.savedLogin.note: Optional. When Discord signs this account out, wumpiary can fill in your credentials automatically. Your email is stored in the encrypted vault; your password is further encrypted under your PIN, so autofill always asks for it first. You still click Log In yourself and handle any captcha or 2FA — nothing is sent automatically.
settings.plugins.title: Plugins
settings.plugins.note: Plugins extend wumpiary's own shell. They run in a sandbox (no Node access) and are disabled by default — you grant each capability individually. Some can access the network, read or write files you select, or run a content script inside Discord (with the high-trust discord-view permission). Drop a plugin folder into the plugins directory and hit Reload. Only enable plugins you trust.
settings.plugins.storageLimit.hint: the most data any single plugin may store
settings.plugins.highTrustTitle: This is a high-trust capability — only grant it to plugins you fully trust.
settings.plugins.noReadme: This plugin hasn't included a README.md.
settings.about.p1: A home for many Wumpuses. Run multiple Discord accounts at once — each in its own isolated, persistent session — and receive notifications from all of them in one place.
settings.about.p2: wumpiary loads the real Discord web client and only observes it; it never automates your accounts or modifies Discord's behaviour.
settings.about.securityNote: Your PIN protects an encrypted vault (scrypt + AES-256-GCM, tied to the OS keychain where available). This guards against casual local access — it's not designed to withstand a determined attacker with full access to a live, running session.
permission.accounts: Read your accounts — nicknames, unread and mention counts, connection state
permission.notifications: See the content of notifications and post its own
permission.discord-css: Inject custom (cosmetic) CSS into the Discord views
permission.discord-view: Run a content script inside the Discord web client — able to read, hide, or extract page content and simulate input (typing, sending, clicking). This is the one capability that can write to Discord.
permission.network: Make outbound network requests, and open connections from its own plugin window (e.g. for streaming or P2P)
permission.files: Open native Save / Open dialogs to read and write files you select each time
permission.filesystem: Read and write files in a private folder this app manages for the plugin — contained, with no access to the rest of your disk
permission.clipboard: Trigger a copy or paste on the focused field (cannot read your clipboard contents)
permission.automation.warning: Account automation is strictly against Discord's terms of service and can lead to permanent account suspension. Please use this plugin responsibly.
menu.forget.title: Remove account
menu.forget.detail: This will wipe its session and remove it from wumpiary.
